name: Syft SBOM Generator (Anchore)
what_it_does: Open-source SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generator with CLI and Go library interface. Generates CycloneDX, SPDX, and custom format SBOMs from container images, filesystems, and source code for supply chain security compliance.
use_cases: Generating SBOMs for container images in CI/CD pipelines for compliance reporting
Producing SPDX and CycloneDX format artifacts for NTIA minimum elements compliance
Integrating with Grype for vulnerability scanning against generated SBOM
Automating SBOM generation and attestation as part of software release workflows
Auditing third-party container images for package inventory and license compliance
not_for: Runtime vulnerability detection — Syft generates static SBOMs, not runtime monitoring
SCA (Software Composition Analysis) with developer IDE integration
Organizations needing commercial support and SLA-backed SBOM tooling
best_when: An agent automates SBOM generation as part of a supply chain security program for container or software artifact compliance.
avoid_when: You need a commercial SBOM platform with dashboards and policy management — use Anchore Enterprise or Snyk instead.

auth.notes: CLI tool with no API server — invoked directly. For container registries, uses standard Docker credential helpers or environment variables. No API key required for core functionality.

pricing.paid_tiers: Anchore Enterprise: commercial SBOM platform with dashboards; pricing on request
pricing.notes: Syft is free open-source Apache 2.0. Anchore Enterprise builds on Syft for enterprise SBOM management. Widely used in CNCF and SLSA compliance programs.

performance.rate_limits: Local execution; limited by container image size and disk I/O

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI interface with --output flag for format selection (json, table, cyclonedx, spdx). JSON output is machine-parseable for agent consumption. Error messages are descriptive. Attestation integration via cosign documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SBOM generation from the same image is deterministic and idempotent. Output format is consistent for same input.
known_agent_gotchas: CLI tool, not REST API — agents must invoke as subprocess or use Go library
Large container images take significant time to analyze — build pipeline timeouts may occur
SBOM accuracy depends on package manager metadata in the image — minimal images may miss packages
CycloneDX and SPDX output formats differ — downstream tools may require specific format

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source — fully auditable. No network service exposed. Supports signed SBOM attestations via cosign. Reads container images locally — no data sent externally. SLSA compliant workflow support.
